briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for April 18, 2026.

sections.title: ⚠️ The Paradox of Domestic Climate Perception and Response Efforts
sections.content: Recently, there have been concerning reports domestically indicating a diminishing perception of the severity of climate change, with an increasing tendency to prioritize personal convenience over environmental protection [1, 4]. Despite this shift in perception, 'Climate Change Week' will be observed from April 20th to 24th, featuring various carbon-neutral initiatives and events in commemoration of Earth Day [2, 3]. Numerous local governments, including Mokpo City, Seongdong-gu, Veterans Health Service, Wonju City, Incheon Jung-gu, and Yangju City, are encouraging citizen participation in carbon-neutral practices through lights-out events and experiential programs [8, 9, 10, 13, 15]. This once again underscores the critical importance of raising public awareness and encouraging practical action in responding to climate change.

sections.title: 🌍 Deepening Global Climate Crisis
sections.content: Globally, the impacts of climate change are intensifying and manifesting in various forms. News that the U.S. Department of Defense is preparing for climate change underscores the severity of the crisis [19], and there are also reports of new antimicrobial threats emerging alongside droughts [23]. Climate change is altering wildfire patterns, with fires that previously subsided at night now continuing to burn through the night [24]. Indigenous plant species are at a crossroads, either adapting or facing extinction due to climate change [21]. Changes in Japan's cherry blossom blooming periods are documenting the history of climate change [22], and even emperor penguins in Antarctica are facing extinction due to climate change and disease [27], indicating widespread threats across ecosystems. With climate experts convening to discuss climate change issues faced by Pakistan [31], this is a critical juncture requiring concerted international response efforts.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Policies and Technological Development Trends
sections.content: Domestically, policy and technological discussions for climate change response are actively underway. Research on climate change response strategies is vibrant, with Chairman Jeong Chan-young integrating and analyzing the three major trends of ESG, carbon neutrality, and climate change into a single system, and related new publications being released [5, 6]. Deep consideration continues on how to resolve the dual challenges of carbon neutrality and energy security [11], and the importance of ecotechnology (eco-friendly technology) is being emphasized as a solution in the era of climate crisis [7, 16]. On a positive note, Cheonan City is gaining momentum in achieving carbon neutrality by reducing 110,000 tons of greenhouse gases [12]. These efforts will form a crucial foundation for strengthening domestic climate change response capabilities.

sections.title: 🌱 Innovation and Cooperation for a Sustainable Future
sections.content: Innovative technological development and international cooperation towards a sustainable future are also showing notable progress. In Europe, a Clean Hydrogen Alliance has been formed, contributing to opening a carbon-neutral future [14], and in the shipping sector, the eco-friendly transition is accelerating, with a German shipbuilding company planning to construct an 82,000-ton, 100% electric cruise ship [18]. Internationally, the Eden Project and a university are renewing their partnership focused on climate change, strengthening research and educational cooperation [32]. Amid ongoing discussions about whether tree planting can still help overcome climate change [29], these multifaceted efforts serve as hopeful signals for overcoming the climate crisis.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights that despite the growing severity of climate change both domestically and internationally, efforts to improve awareness and seek innovative solutions are being pursued concurrently. In this era of climate crisis, we once again emphasize the importance of raising public awareness, technological advancement, and international cooperation, underscoring the need for sustained interest and action. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: A prickle of hedgehogs and an armada of newts: wildlife settles in at London’s new Queen Elizabeth garden
articles.description: A former horticultural nursery in Regent’s Park has been transformed into a diverse mix of habitats, with a wide range of species already spotted ahead of its opening to the public on April 27When the Queen Elizabeth II garden opens in Regent’s Park this month, the first people to visit the Royal Parks’ £5m biodiversity project will quickly discover they are not, in fact, the first visitors.That honour belongs to a hairy-footed flower bee, a breeding pair of geese, some dragonfly nymphs, a flock
